structural_outliers.message: Has 38 files — significantly more than sibling average (7). May be a god-folder that should be split.
Has 28 files — significantly more than sibling average (4). May be a god-folder that should be split.

deep_findings.message: Production application code in a request-error path calls UpdateAppStatus with context.Background() instead of propagating the caller's context, dropping cancellation signals and trace spans.
Dispatch goroutine intentionally starts a new trace root but uses context.Background() as its base; per AGENTS.md, application paths should not introduce context.Background().

deep_findings.evidence: CreateCustomerWithSubject (line 25), GetMeterIDBySlug (line 59), QueryMeterV3 (line 81) each use context.Background() despite receiving *testing.T
deep_findings.message: Test helper functions accept testing.T but create contexts from context.Background() instead of t.Context(), breaking test-scoped lifecycle and cancellation.

deep_findings.evidence: NewServices constructs and exposes FlatFeeService, UsageBasedService, CreditPurchaseService individually (lines 80-85), making it easy for callers to bypass charges.Service
deep_findings.message: The test helper surfaces lower-level sub-services directly alongside the top-level charges.Service, tempting callers to drive lifecycle through the sub-service adapters rather than exclusively through charges.Service.Create / AdvanceCharges / ApplyPatches.

deep_findings.evidence: Identical page-parsing boilerplate (pagination.NewPage(1,20) default, lo.FromPtrOr(params.Page.Number/Size), page.Validate()) duplicated verbatim in billingprofiles/list.go, taxcodes/list.go, and llmcost/list_overrides.go
deep_findings.message: Three v3 list handlers repeat the exact same page-number/size parsing and validation block; this logic should be extracted into a shared request helper (e.g. api/v3/request or pagination package).

deep_findings.evidence: func (a *adapter) buildCreateFlatFeeCharge — uses a.db directly (line 266: a.db.ChargeFlatFee.Create()) without being wrapped by TransactingRepo
deep_findings.message: buildCreateFlatFeeCharge is a shared helper that accesses a.db directly; although it is currently called only from within a TransactingRepo closure (so tx is the receiver), the function signature does not enforce this and future callers on the non-tx adapter would silently bypass transaction rebinding.

deep_findings.evidence: NewCreditGrantService checks billingRegistry.Charges == nil (line 21) but never checks creditsConfig.Enabled before constructing the real creditgrant.Service
deep_findings.message: CreditGrant wiring skips the credits.enabled feature-flag check that all neighbouring providers (ledger.go, customer.go, customerbalance.go) perform, so the credit-grant service will be wired even when credits are explicitly disabled.

deep_findings.evidence: func (h *Handler) Start — line 84: ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
deep_findings.message: Long-lived reconciler goroutine roots its context in context.Background() rather than accepting a lifecycle context from the caller, preventing clean shutdown propagation via context cancellation.
